commentaries.author: Aquinas Study Bible
commentaries.content: washing: that is the washing of Baptism. (St. Anselm)

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: Separated from the world, to which they were once conformed, they pass over into the members of the church. And rightly therefore are they, through whom such things are done, called teeth like to shorn sheep; for they have laid aside the burdens of earthly cares, and coming up from the bath, from the washing away of the filth of the world by the sacrament of baptism, every one bears twins. For they fulfill the two commandments, of which it is said, “On these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets”: loving God with all their heart, and with all their soul and with all their mind, and their neighbor as themselves.

commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: He praises these sheep also in the Song of Solomon, speaking of some perfect ones as the teeth of his spouse the holy church. - "Explanations of the Psalms 95.9"

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
